source_jobs.title: Strategic Projects Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PARETO

Humanity is in a virtuous cycle: human insight improves AI, and better AI expands what people can do. Sustaining it depends on the one input that can't be automated: expert human judgment https://pareto.ai/blog/debating-persuasive-llms-truthful-answers.

At Pareto, we build the platform that turns that judgment into the data https://pareto.ai/blog/community-driven-knowledge-resource-ai, evals https://pareto.ai/blog/introducing-attunebench, and RL environments frontier models https://pareto.ai/blog/llm-metacognition-shared-and-shallow learn from. We work with leading frontier labs like Anthropic and GDM, and we give skilled people everywhere a way to shape the future of AI and share in what it creates.

This RL environment and human-data infrastructure is already in production. Our job now is to scale it.


ABOUT THIS ROLE

Pareto builds human training data pipelines for frontier AI labs. As a Strategic Projects Lead, you sit at the center of that work — owning the architecture, execution, and continuous improvement of complex data collection and evaluation workflows from first scoping call to final delivery.

This is a technical operations role, not a project management role. You'll be expected to read code, deep dive data, reason about LLM internals, design evaluation frameworks, and — increasingly — deploy and iterate on AI agents to automate the work your pipelines do today. We're actively building toward a model where agentic systems handle quality gates, expert routing, and output review, and SPLs are the people designing and operating those systems.

You'll work directly with AI researchers and technical program managers at our client organizations, own delivery against model performance benchmarks, and lead a team of project managers who handle day-to-day execution tracking.



What you'll do

Pipeline architecture Design end-to-end data collection and evaluation pipelines for RLVR, RLHF, SFT, red-teaming, and model evaluation workflows. This includes expert sampling strategy, annotation schema, rubric structure, inter-rater calibration, and QA system design. You'll be expected to prototype novel workflows quickly, identify architectural risks before launch, and make tradeoff decisions with confidence. You’ll also need to understand how agents interact with tools to solve expert-driven tasks, and you’ll need to communicate with engineering to ensure the environment is built accordingly to enable such tasks.

Agentic system deployment Build, test, and iterate on AI agents that automate pipeline tasks — quality gate review, expert matching, output flagging, throughput anomaly detection. You'll work closely with our engineering team to scope agent capabilities, write the prompts and evaluation logic that make them reliable, and monitor their performance in production. This is a growing part of the role; comfort with agentic tooling (LangChain, DSPy, custom tool-use frameworks, or equivalent) is a meaningful differentiator.

Quality systems Define data quality standards across annotation, evaluation, and expert output review. Design and run audits using inter-rater reliability metrics, calibration sets, and statistical sampling. You'll be responsible not just for catching quality issues but for building systems that prevent them — automated checks, structured output validation, and model-assisted review layers where appropriate. Aside from programmatic quality testing, you’ll be responsible for spot-checking tasks & understanding what makes a datapoint meaningful & high quality. The ability to do so, and translate your findings into clear feedback and expert guidelines is particularly helpful for this role.

Client interface Engage directly with AI researchers, TPMs, and PMs at our client organizations. Translate research-driven requirements — evaluation rubrics, domain coverage targets, latency constraints, benchmark specifications — into operational workflows. Communicate pipeline performance clearly, escala

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PARETO

Humanity is in a virtuous cycle: human insight improves AI, and better AI expands what people can do. Sustaining it depends on the one input that can't be automated: expert human judgment https://pareto.ai/blog/debating-persuasive-llms-truthful-answers.

At Pareto, we build the platform that turns that judgment into the data https://pareto.ai/blog/community-driven-knowledge-resource-ai, evals https://pareto.ai/blog/introducing-attunebench, and RL environments frontier models https://pareto.ai/blog/llm-metacognition-shared-and-shallow learn from. We work with leading frontier labs like Anthropic and GDM, and we give skilled people everywhere a way to shape the future of AI and share in what it creates.

This RL environment and human-data infrastructure is already in production. Our job now is to scale it.

Responsibilities

 - Design and build the pipelines that generate synthetic tasks and evaluation environments for AI model training — this is the factory floor of AI development, producing training fuel for next-generation models, not the models themselves

 - Architect the workflows where AI and humans work together in the loop — deciding what gets automated, what requires human intervention, how state is preserved across handoffs, and how the whole system stays reliable at scale

 - Own and lead the most complex system design discussions — produce one-page technical scoping documents that surface hidden risks before development begins, define technology stacks, and establish engineering guidelines that let the team move fast without breaking things

 - Rapidly assess whether a technical idea is worth building — get early signal, align stakeholders, and kill or accelerate accordingly

 - Partner closely with research, operations, and data teams — juggle multiple workstreams, make smart tradeoff decisions as priorities shift, and translate ambiguous business needs into concrete technical architecture

 - Build reusable frameworks and engineering guidelines that raise the team's collective execution muscle



You may be a good fit if you have

 - 8+ years of software engineering experience with a track record of owning complex systems end-to-end

 - A software engineering foundation first — you think in systems, architecture, and engineering tradeoffs, not in models and experiments

 - Production experience building and shipping agentic workflows, multi-agent orchestration, HITL pipelines, and LLM-powered applications with measurable business outcomes — RAG, vector stores, semantic search, and multi-model LLM stacks in production, not just demos

 - Battle-tested context engineering practices — you reason clearly about the limits of AI and architect around them

 - Experience with distributed systems architecture applied to AI or data platforms — reliable, observable, and scalable systems built in service of a product

 - Daily proficiency with agentic coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, or equivalent) — you use these to multiply your output, not pad it

 - A track record of operating in ambiguity — shipping fast, pivoting when wrong, and moving on without ego

 - Exceptional written and verbal English communication skills — you can lead a design discussion, push back on stakeholders, and document architecture clearly. Communication cannot be a bottleneck

 

Nice to Have

 - Experience at an AI data company (Scale AI, Surge, Snorkel, Labelbox, or similar) — particularly building synthetic data pipelines, eval environments, or task generation systems. This is the dream background.

 - Experience building human data labeling interfaces, annotation workflows, or data collection pipelines

 - Familiarity with preference data and reward models used in AI model training (RLHF, RLVR, or similar)

 - Proficiency with our stack: Python, TypeScript, AWS, GCP, Terraform, Temporal Cloud, containerization, LLM gateways, RAG frameworks, and data pipeline tooling

 - Ability to employ data structures and algorithms when forming

source_jobs.title: AI Engagement Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PARETO

Humanity is in a virtuous cycle: human insight improves AI, and better AI expands what people can do. Sustaining it depends on the one input that can't be automated: expert human judgment https://pareto.ai/blog/debating-persuasive-llms-truthful-answers.

At Pareto, we build the platform that turns that judgment into the data https://pareto.ai/blog/community-driven-knowledge-resource-ai, evals https://pareto.ai/blog/introducing-attunebench, and RL environments frontier models https://pareto.ai/blog/llm-metacognition-shared-and-shallow learn from. We work with leading frontier labs like Anthropic and GDM, and we give skilled people everywhere a way to shape the future of AI and share in what it creates.

This RL environment and human-data infrastructure is already in production. Our job now is to scale it.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Pareto is hiring an AI Engagement Manager responsible for owning customer relationships, engagement health, and long-term success across our most strategic AI accounts. This role blends technical credibility with relationship depth — you will manage a portfolio of Tier-1 customers, serving as a trusted advisor to researchers, PMs, and engineering leaders shaping next-generation AI systems. You ensure Pareto remains a valued execution and thought partner across the full customer lifecycle.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Own Strategic Customer Relationships

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for a portfolio of flagship AI lab and model-builder accounts

 - Build multi-threaded relationships across researchers, PMs/TPMs, vendor ops, and procurement

 - Map complex org structures, identify champions, and uncover expansion opportunities

 - Act as a trusted advisor who deeply understands each customer's technical goals and roadmap

Solution Design, Scoping & Commercial Strategy

 - Partner with Product, Delivery, and Engineering to design solutions for Agentic AI, data labeling, evaluation, RLHF, red-teaming, and emerging workflows

 - Ensure strong requirements gathering, alignment on success metrics, and clear translation into operational workflows

 - Maintain standards for scoping, SLAs, quotes, and margins

 - Translate research-driven technical requirements into clear, implementable workflows with Delivery and Product

Account Health, Renewals & Expansion

 - Maintain living account plans outlining use cases, risks, and growth opportunities

 - Drive renewal processes across timing, stakeholder alignment, and messaging

 - Build early-warning systems for churn and coordinate cross-functional mitigation plans

 - Track core account metrics: retention, expansion, account health, and NPS/CSAT

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Partner with Sales on new-logo vs. expansion ownership and seamless handoffs

 - Work closely with Delivery to show up as a unified team to customers

 - Collaborate with Product/R&D to bring structured customer insights into roadmap planning




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - High retention and expansion across your account portfolio with minimal surprise churn

 - Customers who view you as a genuine thought partner, not just a vendor contact

 - Smooth cross-functional execution with Sales and Delivery

 - Repeatable, proactive engagement that surfaces risks and opportunities early




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 4+ years in customer-facing roles with technical buyers (Engagement Management, Strategic Accounts, Customer Success, or product-minded ML/data roles)

 - Experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder accounts (AI labs, model builders, or R&D-heavy orgs preferred)

 - Working knowledge of ML/data workflows (data labeling, evaluation, RLHF, red-teaming, or adjacent)

 - Demonstrated ability to lead solution design and scoping with Delivery/Ops

 - Experience owning or co-owning renewals, expansions, and commercial outcomes

 - Ability to navigate procurement and vendor ops in enterprise settings

 - Excellent communication across researchers, PMs, executives, and procureme

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PARETO

Humanity is in a virtuous cycle: human insight improves AI, and better AI expands what people can do. Sustaining it depends on the one input that can't be automated: expert human judgment https://pareto.ai/blog/debating-persuasive-llms-truthful-answers.

At Pareto, we build the platform that turns that judgment into the data https://pareto.ai/blog/community-driven-knowledge-resource-ai, evals https://pareto.ai/blog/introducing-attunebench, and RL environments frontier models https://pareto.ai/blog/llm-metacognition-shared-and-shallow learn from. We work with leading frontier labs like Anthropic and GDM, and we give skilled people everywhere a way to shape the future of AI and share in what it creates.

This RL environment and human-data infrastructure is already in production. Our job now is to scale it.

Responsibilities:
Working as a Forward Deployed Software Engineer at Pareto AI means partnering directly with labs to understand their challenges and develop targeted solutions while closely supporting them with their goals in AI research and development. Your key duties will include:

 - Leverage innovative thinking, problem-solving capabilities, and engineering expertise to address critical AI challenges for clients

 - Configure and present tailored product demonstrations to highlight platform features for potential clients

 - Advise customers on LLM evaluation best practices and provide strategic AI guidance

 - Write code, construct specialized evaluation datasets, build custom web applications, and produce technical documentation to train customers on platform usage

 - Author technical blog content and promote the product and organization through social platforms and industry gatherings

 - Partner with interdisciplinary teams across delivery, product, engineering, and ML to influence product roadmap and achieve important business objectives
   
    
   
   Qualifications
   
   Most importantly, we seek candidates with initiative, eagerness to grow, unwavering determination, and enthusiasm for direct customer engagement. You would be an excellent candidate if your experience includes:

 - Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or related analytical discipline

 - Solid technical foundation, ideally in machine learning or data analytics

 - Strength in programming languages such as Python, Typescript, or functional languages

 - Familiarity with cloud platforms like AWS

 - Outstanding communication abilities and analytical troubleshooting skills

 - Flexibility to visit customer locations when required (Business trips to California)

 - Having the legal right to travel to the US

 - Demonstrate strong ethics, honesty, and consideration for colleagues

Our stack:

 - Backend:

 - Django 5
   
   1. Python 3.12
   
   2. Celery
   
   3. Redis

 - Frontend:

 - React 18.2

 - Infra (AWS):

 - Fargate
   
   1. ECS
   
   2. RDS

notes: Pareto AI is an AI-focused startup with a strong engineering core, working with leading frontier labs.
